object_under_evaluation: integrity of aoa-memo write-path guardrails on local candidate to reviewed corpus landing routes
claim_boundary: On the currently inspected aoa-memo and abyss-stack surfaces, the reviewed-intake lane now supports a bounded claim that durable landing is gated by reviewed-write posture, source refs, evidence refs, receipt refs, non-untrusted source trust, and authority split, while poisoning subtype coverage remains a next hardening target.
limitations: This report does not prove general memory poisoning resistance.
This report does not prove indirect prompt-injection defense outside tracked memo packet paths.
This report does not prove final truth of the landed memory object.
This report does not prove role authorization outside memo operation mode.
This report does not prove KAG, RAG, vector, or generated retrieval authority.
strongest_guardrail_signal: The reviewed-intake landing tests and audit object show that untrusted candidates, missing source refs, missing evidence refs, missing receipt refs, and candidate-only exports cannot become durable corpus memory.
strongest_write_path_risk: The current lane names indirect prompt injection, sleeper memory, poisoned experience, derivation lineage, and action-safety separation, but not all of those pressures are yet required as first-class packet fields across every local memo port.

case_notes.trust_note: The landing route blocks untrusted or unknown source trust before corpus landing.
case_notes.risk_note: The tested gate covers missing source, evidence, receipt, and reviewed-write posture.
case_notes.lineage_note: The landing receipt and object retain candidate, export, receipt, and source refs.
case_notes.action_note: The script writes a memory object and receipt only; it does not execute source instructions.
case_notes.result_note: The result is a durable aoa-memo source change with schema and landing checks.

case_notes.trust_note: MCP briefs and local ports are named as access or forwarding planes, not reviewed memory authority.
case_notes.risk_note: The threat model names untrusted source text, indirect prompt injection, over-promotion, stale memory, and authority overread.
case_notes.lineage_note: The landed object points back to stack MCP docs, candidate, receipt, export, and reviewed intake refs.
case_notes.action_note: MCP may prepare, validate, search, or dry-run, while durable write stays in aoa-memo.
case_notes.result_note: Authority split is visible enough for bounded consumer review.

case_notes.trust_note: The boundary names source trust and review route as required write-path fields.
case_notes.risk_note: The docs name untrusted source, indirect prompt injection, sleeper memory, poisoned experience, private leakage, stale context, and executable action pressure.
case_notes.lineage_note: Derivation lineage is required by doctrine, but broader packet examples are still needed.
case_notes.action_note: Embedded instructions are explicitly data, not executable action, but action-safety needs wider packet validation.
case_notes.result_note: The guardrail is real and current, while not yet a complete poisoning-resistance suite.
